translated: Yeah, yeah.
Right. Ever since "High Motivation" was announced,
many people said they were excited to see the live performance, so I felt a lot of pressure.
I thought the choreography would be a bit simpler or sweeter,
I really did.
But there's actually a lot of dancing.
Wait, why is that?
I know, right?
It's a fun song, so I was worried I wouldn't be able to express that, but as the live date got closer, all the practice gave me confidence to just enjoy it on the day.
Exactly.
I wanted to show that the 23rd generation alone has the power to pull it off and entertain everyone.
Mm-hmm.
In the end, I was able to have fun, but at first, I couldn't even think about that. I was just desperate. When we first tried singing and dancing at the same time...
Right.
We all said, "This is impossible! This is really bad!"
Even though there are fewer of us in the 23rd generation than the original 11, we couldn't even catch our breath. The formation is so difficult.
And the way they pick up the rhythm isn't just a simple 1-2-3-4.
The choreographer had us moving to even the tiniest sounds in the music.
It really was something, but it turned out great.
Yeah, it did.
So, what part was especially difficult?
From the audience's perspective, as Saku just said, it looks like you're hitting every single beat.
But since we're just watching, we just end up thinking, "That looks like fun!"
Yeah.
Which part was actually hard?
With other songs, I can usually point to a specific part that was tough,
but with this one, every part is a little bit difficult, so it feels like it's hard the whole time.
I see.
Yeah, yeah.
It's just... tough.
How should I put it? There are almost no scenes where you're just standing there,
watching the other girls while it's not your part to sing.
You're always doing one or the other.
We're constantly moving, and while the formations were hard, I was mostly just out of breath from moving so much.
I see.
Yeah, definitely.
I can say it now, but the choreography lessons were pretty intense.
Really?
Was it?
Everyone was crying while we were doing it.
Seriously.
I was a total mess, crying my eyes out.
I really wondered what was going to happen.
You did?
I thought, "Mei-chan would never cry,"
Yeah.
but seeing her cry made me cry even more. I don't even know why.
No, that's not true.
Ah.
Well, I can say it now.
Everyone must have practiced so much at home, because when we met again, the quality was on a whole different level. I was so impressed.
The lyrics even mention something about "having changed," right?
Hearing that now, I think that's what it was referring to.
I wasn't there myself, but...
The song title is "High Motivation," right?
Well...
Even during rehearsals, the 23rd generation would shout,
"High Motivation!" to pump themselves up.
Seeing that, I just thought it was so cool.
Yeah, you really need that kind of motivation to get through a song like that.
But during the actual performance, everyone looked so happy,
and you were all smiling. It made me realize how much hard work must have gone into it.
Everyone worked so hard.
Because everyone gave it their all,
it turned out great.
By the way, did you see Nako's impression?
Wait, what?
I guess you haven't.
You should have her show you sometime.
What is it?
Did you take a video?
No, I didn't, but she's always mimicking the "High Motivation" choreography.
It's so cute, so you should definitely have Nako show you.
I want to see it!
It's a very specific part.
Oh, really?
I want to see.
I wonder which part it is.
Thank you very much.
Thank you.
